title: Optimal Window to Collect Maximum Power

statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.inputFormat: Input will consist of a single line containing a single integer n, followed by n lines containing two space-separated integers, a and b, representing a sensor.
statement.outputFormat: Output will consist of a single line representing the maximum power that can be collected.

examples.explanation: The maximum power that can be collected is 5 + 10 + 17 = 32.

hints.text: The greedy approach is to collect the power from sensors in the order of decreasing power.

editorial.title: Optimal Window to Collect Maximum Power
editorial.intuition: Sort the sensors in decreasing order of power. Then, iterate through the sorted sensors, adding the power of each sensor to the total until the power of the current sensor is less than or equal to the power of the previous sensor.
editorial.bruteForce: The brute force approach is to try all possible windows and calculate the maximum power that can be collected.
editorial.betterSolution: The better solution is to use a greedy approach by sorting the sensors in decreasing order of power.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use a greedy approach by sorting the sensors in decreasing order of power. Then, iterate through the sorted sensors, adding the power of each sensor to the total until the power of the current sensor is less than or equal to the power of the previous sensor.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness of the greedy approach can be proven by showing that the optimal window always has the same size as the window with the maximum power.
editorial.dryRun: Let's dry run the greedy approach on the following example: sensors = [10, 5, 17], initial window = [10], total power = 10.
commonMistakes: Not iterating through the sorted sensors

starterCode.python:
class Solution:
    def maxPower(self, sensors: list[list[int]]):

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def maxPower(self, sensors: list[list[int]]) -> int:
        sensors.sort(key=lambda x: x[0], reverse=True)
        maxPower = 0
        i = 0
        while i < len(sensors) and sensors[i][0] > sensors[i-1][0] if i > 0 else True:
            maxPower += sensors[i][0]
            i += 1
        return maxPower

sensors = [[10, 5], [5, 17], [12, 7]]
print(Solution().maxPower(sensors))
